trap: // Process orders is a terse label, not a doc comment explaining what the module does and how
description: Module/function starts with doc comment: what it does + how it works

trap: // Get user, // Process items, // Update cache describe mechanics, not consequences
description: Comments explain consequences, not mechanics. 'cache so the frontend...' not just 'update cache'

trap: big_orders table and 1000 threshold used without explaining the domain concept
description: Any domain terms explained on first use

trap: // Get user, // Notify, // Check stock are terse mechanical labels
description: Conversational comment tone, not mechanical

trap: throw new Error('blocked') exits early with no explanation of why blocked users are rejected
description: If any no-op/early-return, justified with why

trap: cache.set(`user:${userId}:lastOrder`, ...) with no comment on what next caller sees
description: Cache/DB state mutations explain what next caller sees

trap: return order.rows[0] with no documentation of what the caller should do with it
description: Return value documented: what the caller should do with it

trap: if (total > 1000) has no explanation of why 1000 is the threshold for big orders
description: The 1000 threshold for big orders explained with why

trap: export async function handleOrderData(...) has zero function doc naming consumer-visible behavior
description: Exported function has a function doc (~5 lines max) naming the consumer-visible effect / return-value meaning — NOT a bare label. @example is NOT required (the skill caps function docs at ~5 lines and defaults to no comment).

trap: handleOrderData uses banned words; u, usr, itm, ct, amt, s are cryptic abbreviations
description: handleOrderData renamed — no banned words (handle, data, process). Full words (usr→user, itm→item, ct→count, amt→amount, s→stock)

trap: sendEmail is a boolean without is/has/should prefix
description: Booleans use is/has/should prefix in positive form

trap: cache.set without corresponding cache.get; insert/update naming not symmetric
description: Naming pairs consistent (get/set, add/remove)

trap: isUrgent and sendEmail are boolean params that branch internal behavior
description: isUrgent and sendEmail boolean params split into separate functions or options object

trap: if (itm.qty > 0 && itm.active && !usr.blocked) nested inside for loop with inner if (amt > 0)
description: Nested if/else flattened to guard clauses, max 3 indent levels

trap: discountCode === 'FREE50' ? 50 : discountCode === 'FREE20' ? 20 : 0 nested ternary chain
description: discountCode if/else chain replaced with object map or switch

trap: let total = 0; total += amt; let ct = 0; ct++ mutate accumulators inside loop
description: No mutation of items array or item objects

trap: await db.query('SELECT qty FROM stock...') called sequentially inside for loop
description: Independent stock checks run in parallel (Promise.all)

trap: import { db }, import { cache }, import { mailer } are hardcoded global imports
description: db, cache, mailer injected via factory, not imported as globals

trap: handleOrderData mixes 7 concerns in one function: fetch user, loop-price items, check stock, big-order notification, create order, set priority, send email, update cache, log
description: handleOrderData is split by RESPONSIBILITY (not line count): at minimum fetch user, price items, persist order, and notify are separate named operations. The split is driven by mixing concerns, not by hitting an arbitrary line threshold.

trap: handleOrderData(userId, items, isUrgent, sendEmail, emailTo, taxRate, discountCode) has 7 positional args
description: 7 positional args replaced with options object

trap: Order creation, email sending, cache update, and logging all in one function
description: Order creation (command) separated from notification (side-effect)

trap: 1000, 50, 20 are magic numbers used inline without named constants
description: Magic numbers (1000, 50, 20) extracted to named constants

trap: userId: any, items: any[] accept anything; OrderStatus = string is untyped
description: Explicit types/interfaces for all params and returns, no any

trap: 'FREE50', 'FREE20' discount codes and 1000 threshold hardcoded in function body
description: Discount codes/thresholds in config, not inline

trap: No validation on qty (could be negative), items array (could be empty)
description: Input validation rejects invalid values (negative qty, empty items) — no ?? fallback

trap: throw new Error('not found'), throw new Error('blocked'), throw new Error('stock')
description: Errors returned as Result, no throw

trap: throw new Error('stock') is a bare string with no original cause or context
description: Errors preserve original cause/context

trap: itm.qty * itm.price * (taxRate ? 1 + taxRate : 1) - (discountCode === ...) is one compound expression
description: Compound expressions (tax calc, discount) extracted to named variables

trap: discountCode === 'FREE50' ? 50 : discountCode === 'FREE20' ? 20 : 0 nested ternary
description: Nested ternary for discounts removed, replaced with clear logic

trap: Dense code blocks with inconsistent spacing between logical sections
description: Logical blocks separated with blank lines

trap: // Log inside business logic comment labels dead-weight code that should be removed
description: Commented-out old approach removed

trap: No existing TODOs but refactoring should not introduce any without issue links
description: TODO/FIXME without tracking issue removed

trap: if (isUrgent)/else and if (sendEmail) branches have no journey narration
description: If any conditional branches, narrated with journey (optional — depends on refactoring approach)

trap: OrderStatus = string instead of union type; status 'pending' is a raw string
description: Make invalid states unrepresentable — union types for status, not bare strings

trap: userId: any, items: any[] — accepted without parsing into typed domain objects
description: Parse, don't validate — construct typed domain objects at boundary, reject invalid

trap: db.query calls have no timeout — can hang forever
description: Timeout on every I/O call

trap: console.log('Order created') inside business logic
description: No logging in business logic — use crosscutting wrapper (withLogging/withTracing)

trap: throw new Error('not found') — bare string, no type/code/status
description: Structured errors: { type, code, status, detail } not bare { message }

trap: return order.rows[0] — raw DB row returned to caller
description: Map DB entities to DTOs — dedicated response type, not raw rows

trap: Single export mixed in with all implementation
description: Exports/public API at top of file, private helpers at bottom

trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice are 3 functions with identical params (to, name, amount, currency) and similar bodies — a pattern has emerged
description: 3 similar functions with identical signatures extracted into a shared abstraction or parameterized function

trap: to, name, amount, currency appear as the same 4 params across formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice
description: Repeated param group (to, name, amount, currency) extracted to a Value Object / named type

trap: order.customer.address.street, order.customer.address.city, order.customer.address.zip — 3 levels deep into object graph
description: Chained property access 2+ levels deep replaced with direct accessor or destructured at boundary

trap: validateOrder mixes high-level validation (check city exists, check total) with low-level string formatting (template literal with toFixed)
description: validateOrder split — validation logic separated from string formatting, no mixing of abstraction levels

trap: removeCartItem throws 'item not in cart' when removing an item that doesn't exist — but the postcondition is the same: item is not in cart
description: removeCartItem redesigned to succeed even if item absent — guarantees 'after call, item is not in cart' instead of throwing

trap: applyBulkDiscount forces callers to pass retryCount, retryDelayMs, maxRetries — retry logic pushed to caller instead of absorbed internally
description: Retry config params removed from applyBulkDiscount — module handles retry internally or removes it (caller shouldn't deal with retry complexity)

trap: if (total <= 0) throw new Error('invalid total') in validateOrder — a negative total after summing valid items is a programmer bug, but the code treats it like a normal error with no explanation
description: Impossible-state check returns Result error (not throw) AND has a comment explaining why this state should never occur — the comment documents the invariant

trap: Validation scattered: usr.blocked checked in main function AND redundantly inside loop (itm.qty > 0 && itm.active && !usr.blocked)
description: Validation consolidated at boundary — usr.blocked checked once at entry (barricade), not re-checked inside loop

trap: The order pipeline has 5+ sequential steps (validate user → check stock → calculate total → create order → set priority → notify → cache) explained only as a dense prose paragraph or not at all
description: The multi-step order pipeline (3+ steps) is documented as a module/function doc that lists the steps as a bullet list / step sequence (per the skill's mandatory module-doc shape: what, consumer consequence, how-as-bullets). An ASCII diagram is NOT required — the skill defaults to no comment and uses bullet lists, not diagrams.

trap: The top-of-file comment says 'Was originally a class-based OrderService, refactored to functions in Q3' — it narrates past implementation history
description: The 'refactored from a class in Q3' archaeology comment is removed. No comment references past implementation (no 'previously', 'used to', 'was', 'before', 'originally', 'refactored from')

trap: getInstanceConfig has a comment 'These values are exposed because the admin dashboard needs them on the instance settings page' — a shared API comment anchored to one specific caller (the admin dashboard / instance settings page) instead of the stable domain purpose
description: getInstanceConfig comment rewritten to describe the stable domain purpose of the config values (what they mean / what consumers decide with them) — NO reference to the admin dashboard, the instance settings page, or any single caller

trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice, getShippingLabel, validateOrder, applyBulkDiscount, handleOrderData all mixed together — order fulfillment, document formatting, shipping, and validation are multiple unrelated business domains
description: The unrelated business domains are separated into clearly delimited sections/modules — order fulfillment, document formatting, shipping, validation are NOT interleaved or fused into one function. Since the prompt mandates all code inline in one file (no file writes), separation via distinct named sections + a noted split boundary (e.g. a comment 'belongs in its own module: shipping') counts; actual file split is NOT required here.
